title: generate kube -s: do not emit ports.targetPort equal to ports.port
body:
I use podman at commit 171f7b8975194ff9f47a.
```
$ podman generate kube -s <ID>
# Created with podman-4.0.0-dev
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
spec:        
  ports:           
  - name: "3890"     
    nodePort: 31622      
    port: 3890
    targetPort: 3890
```
According to the reference of Service.spec.ports - https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/kubernetes-api/service-resources/service-v1/#ServiceSpec- missing ports.targetPort implies the same value as ports.port.  So there is no point to render targetPort in this case.

comments.body:
So even though the `TargetPort` option in the Service struct is set to omitempty, it still automatically sets it to 0 when we leave it as empty - no idea why that is happening
```
	TargetPort intstr.IntOrString `json:"targetPort,omitempty" protobuf:"bytes,4,opt,name=targetPort"`

```

That is why I decided it was best to just make its value the same as ports.port so it doesn't end up with a 0 value. Here is the patch that fixed that https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11982/files.
Does the port expected to be nil instead of 0?
Yeah, if it is not set it should be empty and the field shouldn't show up in the yaml. However, when it is empty or not set it is equating that to 0 and it is showing up in the generated yaml.
Closing this as targetPort gets the value of 0 when it is left empty and is coming from where the struct is defined in k8s.
